chunks.text: MFI evolution - 02 Gamma 02 Gamma (γ) Date of Fabrication February 2002 Close up of the wing differential on 02-γ Description 02-gamma was the second wing differential mounted on a flight mill after the first attempt (01-θ) had a number of problems w

he first attempt (01-θ) had a number of problems with alignment, fabrication, etc. 02-γ was completed with little fabrication problems and consequently, it was possible to generate varying thrust vectors by changing the inputs to the 2 piezos. The fo

ors by changing the inputs to the 2 piezos. The following movies show how the wing mounted at the flight mill moves in different directions based on the phase difference to the 2 piezos. MFI moving to the left MFI again moving to the left MFI moving

the left MFI again moving to the left MFI moving down MFI moving up MFI again moving down Motion of MFI wing seen under strobe lights Long shot of the MFI moving to the top and left Fabrication Parameters Fourbar Dimensions See solidworks drawings f

eters Fourbar Dimensions See solidworks drawings for complete dimensions. Link lengths (mm) 6, 6, 6, 1 Flexure lengths (μm) 50, 50, 112.5, 112.5 Flexure widths (mm) 3,2,2,2.5 Slider crank dimensions Attachment point (mm) 1.5 Wing Differential Dimensi

ttachment point (mm) 1.5 Wing Differential Dimensions See solidworks drawings for complete dimensions. Leading and lagging spar lengths (mm) 2,3 Leading and lagging spar inertias (mg-mm^2) 2,3 Distance between spars (mm) 1 Differential Transmission r
